title: tephrosia obovata
intro: Tephrosia obovata, also known as the "fish poison tree," is a species of flowering plant in the pea family, Fabaceae. This plant is used in traditional medicine for its various medicinal properties, including its ability to lower blood sugar levels and reduce inflammation. The health benefits of Tephrosia obovata include its potential to treat diabetes, arthritis, and other inflammatory conditions, as well as its antimicrobial and antifungal properties. Herbal preparations of Tephrosia obovata are typically made from the plant's leaves and roots, and are often used in the form of teas, tinctures, or capsules.
